# personal_db data contract

<!-- Auto-generated by `personal-db dev contract --bundled`. Do not edit
     by hand — regenerate with:
       personal-db dev contract --bundled --output docs/data-contract.md
-->

This document describes every bundled tracker, app, and source: what tables
it puts in `db.sqlite`, what each column means, and how fresh/complete the
data is expected to be.

## Consumer rules

- Read `db.sqlite` freely (read-only connection); never write to it.
- Mutations go through the daemon HTTP API (`/api/v1`, Bearer token from
  `<root>/state/daemon.token`).
- Table shapes are versioned per-tracker via `schema_version`; check
  `tracker_schema_versions`.

## Core tables

These tables exist in every `db.sqlite` regardless of which trackers/apps are
installed (see `core/db.py` and `core/enrichment_queue.py`):

- **`people`** / **`people_aliases`** — resolved people and the raw
  identifiers (handles, emails, aliases) that map onto them.
- **`notes`** — an index over the user's notes directory (`path`, `title`,
  `created_at`, `body_excerpt`); the note bodies themselves live as files
  under `<root>/notes`, not in the DB.
- **`tracker_schema_versions`** — one row per tracker, the `schema_version`
  currently applied to its tables. Compare against a tracker's manifest
  `schema_version` before assuming its table shapes match this document.
- **`action_log`** — an audit trail of every action dispatched through the
  daemon (`surface`, `extension`, `action`, `params_json`, `result`),
  written before each action runs.
- **`enrichment_jobs`** (+ `enrichment_runs` / `enrichment_latest` /
  `enrichment_evidence`) — the generic background-enrichment queue: pending/
  running/completed jobs, their results, and cited evidence. Finance receipt
  enrichment is the first thing built on top of it.


## Trackers (26)

### `calendar`

macOS Calendar events and planned-vs-actual reality blocks

- **permission_type**: `full_disk_access`
- **schema_version**: `1`
- **platform: macOS only**
- **local_only**: this tracker reads from local files/apps that don't survive a reinstall — expect a data horizon (no rows before the first successful sync on this machine) rather than a complete history.

#### Table `calendar_events`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `event_id` | `TEXT` | stable event id derived from source database and event UID/primary key |
| `source` | `TEXT` | source adapter, currently macos_calendar |
| `source_db` | `TEXT` | calendar source database path |
| `source_pk` | `TEXT` | source row primary key |
| `calendar_id` | `TEXT` | source calendar id when available |
| `calendar_title` | `TEXT` | calendar name when available |
| `title` | `TEXT` | event title |
| `location` | `TEXT` | event location |
| `notes_hash` | `TEXT` | sha256 hash of notes/description text, not the text itself |
| `start_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC event start |
| `end_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC event end |
| `all_day` | `INTEGER` | 1 when all-day event |
| `timezone` | `TEXT` | source timezone identifier when available |
| `url` | `TEXT` | event URL when available |
| `status` | `TEXT` | source status when available |
| `availability` | `TEXT` | source availability/free-busy value when available |
| `imported_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC import time |

#### Table `calendar_reality_blocks`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `event_id` | `TEXT` | calendar_events.event_id |
| `date` | `TEXT` | local date YYYY-MM-DD |
| `title` | `TEXT` | event title |
| `calendar_title` | `TEXT` | calendar title |
| `start_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC event start |
| `end_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC event end |
| `planned_minutes` | `INTEGER` | scheduled event duration |
| `actual_minutes` | `INTEGER` | best available active-computer minutes overlapping event |
| `screen_time_minutes` | `INTEGER` | overlapping macOS Screen Time app minutes |
| `mosspath_events` | `INTEGER` | Mosspath Lite event count during block |
| `chrome_visits` | `INTEGER` | Chrome visit count during block |
| `app_count` | `INTEGER` | distinct observed foreground apps |
| `domain_count` | `INTEGER` | distinct observed browser domains |
| `top_apps_json` | `TEXT` | JSON ranked app observations during the event |
| `top_domains_json` | `TEXT` | JSON ranked browser domains during the event |

- **time_column**: `start_at`
- **granularity**: `event`

### `code_agent_activity`

Coding-agent runtime state — Claude Code (via hooks) and Codex CLI (via rollout JSONL). Records session-level intervals (agent_running, awaiting_user, inactive) so efficiency, engagement, and prompt-cadence questions are answerable without leaving db.sqlite.

- **permission_type**: `none`
- **schema_version**: `1`
- **platform: macOS only**
- **local_only**: this tracker reads from local files/apps that don't survive a reinstall — expect a data horizon (no rows before the first successful sync on this machine) rather than a complete history.

#### Table `code_agent_events`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `agent` | `TEXT` | agent name, e.g. claude_code or codex |
| `session_id` | `TEXT` | agent-assigned session identifier |
| `timestamp` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC event timestamp |
| `event_type` | `TEXT` | state-transition event type, e.g. session_start, user_prompt_submit, stop |
| `cwd` | `TEXT` | working directory at event time |
| `git_branch` | `TEXT` | git branch at event time when available |
| `source_file` | `TEXT` | originating hook payload file or rollout JSONL path |
| `raw` | `TEXT` | original hook payload or rollout line JSON when store_raw is true |

#### Table `code_agent_intervals`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `agent` | `TEXT` | agent name, e.g. claude_code or codex |
| `session_id` | `TEXT` | agent-assigned session identifier |
| `start_ts` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC interval start |
| `end_ts` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC interval end |
| `state` | `TEXT` | interval state: agent_running, awaiting_user, or inactive |
| `duration_seconds` | `REAL` | interval duration in seconds |
| `cwd` | `TEXT` | working directory at interval start |
| `git_branch` | `TEXT` | git branch at interval start when available |

#### Table `code_agent_sessions`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `agent` | `TEXT` | agent name, e.g. claude_code or codex_cli |
| `session_id` | `TEXT` | agent-assigned session identifier |
| `cwd` | `TEXT` | absolute working directory; resolved from JSONL message metadata or hook events |
| `started_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC, earliest user/assistant message timestamp |
| `last_msg_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC, latest user/assistant message timestamp |
| `message_count` | `INTEGER` | user + assistant messages |
| `user_msg_count` | `INTEGER` | user messages |
| `assistant_msg_count` | `INTEGER` | assistant messages |
| `first_user_prompt` | `TEXT` | first user message text, truncated to 500 chars |
| `source_file` | `TEXT` | absolute path of originating JSONL |

- **time_column**: `timestamp`
- **granularity**: `event`

### `contacts`

macOS Contacts (AddressBook) — names, phones, emails, and Telegram URLs. Reference table for joining against imessage handles, calendar attendees, etc.

- **permission_type**: `manual`
- **schema_version**: `1`
- **local_only**: this tracker reads from local files/apps that don't survive a reinstall — expect a data horizon (no rows before the first successful sync on this machine) rather than a complete history.

#### Table `contact_handles`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `contact_id` | `TEXT` | FK to contacts.contact_id |
| `kind` | `TEXT` | "phone", "email", or "telegram" |
| `normalized` | `TEXT` | normalized for matching — lowercased email, last-10-digits of phone, or stable Telegram identifier |
| `raw` | `TEXT` | original handle as stored in AddressBook |

#### Table `contacts`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `contact_id` | `TEXT` | stable id "<source>:<source_pk>" — unique across AddressBook source DBs |
| `full_name` | `TEXT` | "first last" — empty if both missing |
| `display_name` | `TEXT` | best human-readable label (full_name → nickname → organization → "(unnamed)") |
| `organization` | `TEXT` | organization / company name if any |
| `source` | `TEXT` | AddressBook source identifier (UUID for sub-source DBs, or "main") |
| `updated_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 of last modification in the source DB (or sync time if unknown) |

- **time_column**: `updated_at`
- **granularity**: `event`

#### Table `github_commits`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `sha` | `TEXT` | commit SHA, primary key |
| `repo` | `TEXT` | owner/name |

| `message` | `TEXT` | first line of commit message |
| `additions` | `INTEGER` | lines added |
| `deletions` | `INTEGER` | lines deleted |

- **time_column**: `committed_at`

### `granola`

Granola meeting notes — title, content, transcript, participants

- **permission_type**: `api_key`
- **schema_version**: `1`
- **platform: macOS only**

#### Table `granola_documents`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `TEXT` | Granola document id, primary key |
| `started_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 meeting start (UTC), from first transcript utterance, falls back to created_at |
| `finished_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 meeting end (UTC), from last transcript utterance |

| `title` | `TEXT` | Granola-generated meeting title |

| `content` | `TEXT` | raw Granola notes JSON (ProseMirror-style) |
| `transcript` | `TEXT` | speaker-attributed transcript, one line per utterance: '[me] ...' / '[them] ...' |
| `participants` | `TEXT` | JSON array of meeting participants |
| `created_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 doc creation (≠ meeting start) |
| `updated_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 last edit |

- **time_column**: `started_at`
- **granularity**: `event`

### `instagram`

Instagram Reels analytics (views, reach, engagement) via the Graph API

- **schema_version**: `1`

#### Table `instagram_account_snapshots`

| column | type | semantic |

| `snapshot_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 of when this account snapshot was taken |
| `username` | `TEXT` | Instagram username at snapshot time |
| `name` | `TEXT` | Profile display name when returned by the API |
| `account_type` | `TEXT` | BUSINESS / MEDIA_CREATOR / PERSONAL when returned by the API |
| `media_count` | `INTEGER` | Total media count at snapshot time |
| `followers_count` | `INTEGER` | Follower count at snapshot time |
| `follows_count` | `INTEGER` | Following count at snapshot time |
| `raw_json` | `TEXT` | Raw profile response JSON for future field compatibility |

#### Table `reels_insights_snapshots`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `media_id` | `TEXT` | FK to reels_media.media_id |
| `snapshot_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 of when this snapshot was taken |
| `views` | `INTEGER` | plays on Instagram (replaces deprecated `plays`) |
| `reach` | `INTEGER` | unique accounts that saw it (estimated) |
| `likes` | `INTEGER` | like count |
| `comments` | `INTEGER` | comment count |
| `shares` | `INTEGER` | share count |
| `saved` | `INTEGER` | save count |
| `reposts` | `INTEGER` | reposts minus deleted reposts |
| `total_interactions` | `INTEGER` | likes+saves+comments+shares net of undo/delete |
| `ig_reels_avg_watch_time_ms` | `INTEGER` | average watch time across viewers (ms) |
| `ig_reels_video_view_total_time_ms` | `INTEGER` | total watch time across all viewers (ms) |
| `reels_skip_rate_pct` | `REAL` | % who skipped within first 3s (estimated, in development) |
| `crossposted_views` | `INTEGER` | plays aggregated across IG + FB |
| `facebook_views` | `INTEGER` | plays on Facebook (when crossposted) |

#### Table `reels_media`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `media_id` | `TEXT` | Instagram media ID, primary key |
| `ig_user_id` | `TEXT` | Instagram business/creator user ID that owns the media |
| `media_product_type` | `TEXT` | REELS | FEED | STORY | AD — we only ingest REELS but store all so cursor walks work |
| `media_type` | `TEXT` | VIDEO | IMAGE | CAROUSEL_ALBUM |
| `permalink` | `TEXT` | instagram.com canonical URL |
| `caption` | `TEXT` | post caption (may be long; IG may truncate server-side) |
| `thumbnail_url` | `TEXT` | CDN thumbnail URL (expires; refetch if needed) |
| `timestamp` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 when the reel was posted (UTC) |
| `fetched_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 when first ingested into personal_db |

- **time_column**: `timestamp`
- **granularity**: `event`

### `life_context`

Lightweight diary — structured "state" tags + free-text notes, day-granular.
Manual capture only. Use it to annotate days that need explanation when other
trackers look weird (sick stretch, travel, system reinstall, vacation, etc.)
so future-you (or future-Claude) reads the data correctly.


- **permission_type**: `none`
- **schema_version**: `1`
- **platform: macOS only**
- **local_only**: this tracker reads from local files/apps that don't survive a reinstall — expect a data horizon (no rows before the first successful sync on this machine) rather than a complete history.

#### Table `life_context`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `INTEGER` | autoincrement primary key |
| `date` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 local date YYYY-MM-DD this entry applies to |
| `state` | `TEXT` | optional categorical tag (sick, traveling, well, etc.) |
| `note` | `TEXT` | free-text annotation explaining what was happening that day |
| `logged_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 timestamp of when the entry was created |

- **time_column**: `date`
- **granularity**: `day`

### `notifications`

macOS notification deliveries and inferred attention impact

- **permission_type**: `full_disk_access`
- **schema_version**: `1`
- **platform: macOS only**
- **local_only**: this tracker reads from local files/apps that don't survive a reinstall — expect a data horizon (no rows before the first successful sync on this machine) rather than a complete history.

#### Table `notification_impacts`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `notification_id` | `TEXT` | source_record_id from notifications_events |
| `delivered_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC notification delivery time |
| `bundle_id` | `TEXT` | sender app bundle identifier |
| `app_name` | `TEXT` | sender app display name |
| `impact` | `TEXT` | ignored, glanced, acted_on, derailed, or batched |
| `confidence` | `REAL` | heuristic confidence, 0-1 |
| `evidence` | `TEXT` | short explanation of nearby activity evidence |
| `batch_count` | `INTEGER` | same-source notifications in the surrounding burst window |
| `prior_event_id` | `TEXT` | nearest Mosspath Lite event before delivery |
| `prior_at` | `TEXT` | prior event timestamp |
| `prior_app_name` | `TEXT` | prior foreground app name |
| `prior_bundle_id` | `TEXT` | prior foreground app bundle id |
| `next_event_id` | `TEXT` | nearest Mosspath Lite event after delivery |
| `next_at` | `TEXT` | next event timestamp |
| `next_app_name` | `TEXT` | next foreground app name |
| `next_bundle_id` | `TEXT` | next foreground app bundle id |
| `acted_at` | `TEXT` | first nearby foreground event matching the notification source |
| `seconds_to_action` | `INTEGER` | seconds between delivery and inferred action |
| `returned_at` | `TEXT` | first return to prior context after action |
| `away_seconds` | `INTEGER` | seconds away from prior context before return or analysis window end |
| `computed_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC computation time |

#### Table `notifications_events`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `source_record_id` | `TEXT` | stable source row identifier from the macOS usernoted record table |
| `delivered_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC notification delivery time |
| `bundle_id` | `TEXT` | sender app bundle identifier |
| `app_name` | `TEXT` | sender app display name when resolvable |
| `title` | `TEXT` | notification title, only populated when content storage is explicitly enabled |
| `subtitle` | `TEXT` | notification subtitle, only populated when content storage is explicitly enabled |
| `body` | `TEXT` | notification body, only populated when content storage is explicitly enabled |
| `title_hash` | `TEXT` | sha256 hash of notification title |
| `subtitle_hash` | `TEXT` | sha256 hash of notification subtitle |
| `body_hash` | `TEXT` | sha256 hash of notification body |
| `content_hash` | `TEXT` | sha256 hash across bundle, title, subtitle, and body |
| `thread_id` | `TEXT` | notification thread identifier when present |
| `category_id` | `TEXT` | notification category identifier when present |
| `request_id` | `TEXT` | notification request identifier when present |
| `source` | `TEXT` | source adapter, currently usernoted |
| `imported_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC import time |

- **time_column**: `delivered_at`
- **granularity**: `event`

### `project_time`

Per-day, per-project time breakdown derived from coding sessions (Claude Code, Codex CLI),
GitHub commits, and screen time. Projects are defined in projects.yaml and matched against
repo names, code agent working directories, and macOS app bundle_ids. Hours are
attributed to the date a session started (local time). Single-session contributions are
capped at 4 hours to avoid stale "left the terminal open" sessions inflating totals.


- **permission_type**: `none`
- **schema_version**: `1`

#### Table `project_time`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `date` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 local date YYYY-MM-DD |
| `project` | `TEXT` | project name from projects.yaml |
| `hours` | `REAL` | total attributed hours on this date for this project (capped per-session) |
| `commit_count` | `INTEGER` | number of github commits authored to a matching repo on this date |
| `breakdown_json` | `TEXT` | JSON like {"claude": 1.2, "codex": 0.4, "app": 0.3} — sums to hours |

- **time_column**: `date`
- **granularity**: `day`

### `screen_time`

macOS app usage from ~/Library/Application Support/Knowledge/knowledgeC.db

- **permission_type**: `full_disk_access`
- **schema_version**: `1`
- **platform: macOS only**
- **local_only**: this tracker reads from local files/apps that don't survive a reinstall — expect a data horizon (no rows before the first successful sync on this machine) rather than a complete history.

#### Table `screen_time_app_usage`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `INTEGER` | rowid |
| `bundle_id` | `TEXT` | macOS bundle identifier (e.g. com.apple.dt.Xcode) |
| `start_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC |
| `end_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 UTC |
| `seconds` | `INTEGER` | duration in seconds |

- **time_column**: `start_at`
- **granularity**: `event`

| `zone_one_milli` | `INTEGER` | time in HR zone 1 ms |
| `zone_two_milli` | `INTEGER` | time in HR zone 2 ms |
| `zone_three_milli` | `INTEGER` | time in HR zone 3 ms |
| `zone_four_milli` | `INTEGER` | time in HR zone 4 ms |
| `zone_five_milli` | `INTEGER` | time in HR zone 5 ms |

- **time_column**: `start`
- **granularity**: `day`

### `withings`

Withings smart-scale body composition (weight, fat %, muscle, bone, etc.)

- **permission_type**: `oauth`
- **schema_version**: `1`

#### Table `withings_measurements`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `grpid` | `TEXT` | Withings measure-group id, primary key |
| `date` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 measurement time (UTC) |
| `timezone` | `TEXT` | IANA tz reported by Withings (e.g. America/Los_Angeles); per-row because user travels |
| `attrib` | `INTEGER` | 0=device user known, 1=device ambiguous, 2=manual entry, 4=manual ambiguous, 5=user-create, 7=auto, 8=device hash unknown |
| `category` | `INTEGER` | 1=real measurement, 2=user objective |
| `device_id` | `TEXT` | Withings deviceid (raw, may be empty for manual entries) |
| `weight_kg` | `REAL` | weight in kilograms (Withings type 1) |
| `fat_ratio_pct` | `REAL` | body fat percentage (type 6) |
| `fat_mass_kg` | `REAL` | fat mass in kg (type 8) |
| `lean_mass_kg` | `REAL` | fat-free mass in kg (type 5) |
| `muscle_mass_kg` | `REAL` | muscle mass in kg (type 76); often equals lean mass on basic scales |
| `bone_mass_kg` | `REAL` | bone mass in kg (type 88) |
| `hydration_kg` | `REAL` | body water in kg (type 77) |
| `heart_pulse_bpm` | `INTEGER` | pulse at weigh-in (type 11); only on Body Cardio / Body Comp / Body Smart |
| `created_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 record creation time (UTC) |
| `modified_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 last modification time (UTC); used for cursor |

- **time_column**: `date`
- **granularity**: `event`

### `xhs`

Xiaohongshu / RedNote own-post status snapshots from a logged-in Chrome session

- **permission_type**: `manual`
- **schema_version**: `1`
- **platform: macOS only**

#### Table `xhs_account_snapshots`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `profile_url` | `TEXT` | Source URL for the account-level snapshot |
| `snapshot_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 of when this profile snapshot was taken |
| `nickname` | `TEXT` | Profile nickname when visible |
| `following_count` | `INTEGER` | Visible following count |
| `followers_count` | `INTEGER` | Visible follower count |
| `liked_collected_count` | `INTEGER` | Visible aggregate likes+collects count when shown |
| `visible_note_count` | `INTEGER` | Number of active notes discovered in creator note-manager during this sync |
| `raw_json` | `TEXT` | Best-effort raw creator note-manager summary |

#### Table `xhs_note_snapshots`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `note_id` | `TEXT` | FK to xhs_notes.note_id |
| `snapshot_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 of when this status snapshot was taken |
| `view_count` | `INTEGER` | Creator-center view/read count |
| `liked_count` | `INTEGER` | Public like count |
| `collected_count` | `INTEGER` | Public collect/save count |
| `comment_count` | `INTEGER` | Public comment count |
| `share_count` | `INTEGER` | Public share count when exposed |
| `raw_json` | `TEXT` | Compact raw note/status JSON for future parser compatibility |

#### Table `xhs_notes`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `note_id` | `TEXT` | Xiaohongshu note id, primary key |
| `xhs_user_id` | `TEXT` | Author user id when available |
| `author_nickname` | `TEXT` | Author display nickname at latest fetch |
| `note_type` | `TEXT` | Normal, video, or other platform note type when available |
| `title` | `TEXT` | Note title or display title |
| `description` | `TEXT` | Note body/description text |
| `permalink` | `TEXT` | Canonical Xiaohongshu note URL inferred from the creator-center note id |
| `thumbnail_url` | `TEXT` | First image/thumbnail URL when available |
| `posted_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 note publish time; first_seen_at fallback if unavailable |
| `visibility_label` | `TEXT` | Creator-center visibility label, e.g. public/private/archive text when visible |
| `is_archived` | `INTEGER` | 1 when creator center marks the note private/archived so active analytics can exclude it |
| `creator_last_seen_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 when the note was last seen in creator note-manager |
| `first_seen_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 when personal_db first discovered this note |
| `last_fetched_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 when metadata was last refreshed |

- **time_column**: `posted_at`
- **granularity**: `event`

### `xhs_saved`

Xiaohongshu / RedNote saved posts collected from a logged-in Chrome session

- **permission_type**: `manual`
- **schema_version**: `1`
- **platform: macOS only**

#### Table `xhs_saved_collections`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `collected_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 time when the browser collection finished |
| `source_url` | `TEXT` | Profile or active tab URL used for collection |
| `source_title` | `TEXT` | Browser page title at collection time |
| `clicked_saved_tab` | `INTEGER` | 1 when the 收藏 tab click was detected |
| `note_count` | `INTEGER` | Number of unique saved note links discovered |
| `raw_json` | `TEXT` | Compact raw collection metadata, excluding cookies |

#### Table `xhs_saved_post_snapshots`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `note_id` | `TEXT` | FK to xhs_saved_posts.note_id |
| `snapshot_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 of when this public-count snapshot was taken |
| `liked_count` | `INTEGER` | Public like count |
| `collected_count` | `INTEGER` | Public collect/save count |
| `comment_count` | `INTEGER` | Public comment count |
| `share_count` | `INTEGER` | Public share count when exposed |
| `raw_json` | `TEXT` | Compact raw interaction JSON |

#### Table `xhs_saved_posts`

| column | type | semantic |
|---|---|---|
| `note_id` | `TEXT` | Xiaohongshu note id, primary key |
| `source_url` | `TEXT` | Latest saved-note URL, usually including xsec parameters |
| `first_seen_url` | `TEXT` | First saved-note URL observed by personal_db |
| `xsec_token` | `TEXT` | xsec_token query parameter from the saved link when present |
| `xsec_source` | `TEXT` | xsec_source query parameter from the saved link when present |
| `title` | `TEXT` | Note title or display title |
| `description` | `TEXT` | Note body/description text |
| `author_user_id` | `TEXT` | Author user id when available |
| `author_nickname` | `TEXT` | Author display nickname at latest fetch |
| `note_type` | `TEXT` | Normal, video, or other platform note type when available |
| `posted_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 note publish time when available |
| `thumbnail_url` | `TEXT` | First image/thumbnail URL when available |
| `image_urls_json` | `TEXT` | JSON array of image URLs from note detail |
| `video_urls_json` | `TEXT` | JSON array of video URLs from note detail |
| `saved_first_seen_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 when personal_db first discovered this saved post |
| `saved_last_seen_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 when personal_db last saw this post in 收藏 |
| `latest_seen_rank` | `INTEGER` | Most recent observed position in the saved grid; 1 is newest/first |
| `last_fetched_at` | `TEXT` | ISO-8601 when note detail was last refreshed |
| `fetch_status` | `TEXT` | ok, pending, or error for the latest detail fetch |
| `fetch_error` | `TEXT` | Latest detail fetch error message, if any |
| `raw_json` | `TEXT` | Compact raw note/detail JSON for parser compatibility |

- **time_column**: `saved_first_seen_at`
- **granularity**: `event`

## Apps (5)

### `attention`

Notification impact workspace for finding interruptions, ignored noise, and derailment patterns.

- **reads**: tables: notifications_events, notification_impacts, mosspath_lite_events
- **pages**: overview, sources, events

### `calendar_reality`

Planned calendar time compared with actual app, browser, and project activity.

- **reads**: tables: calendar_events, calendar_reality_blocks, mosspath_lite_events, screen_time_app_usage, chrome_visits, project_time
- **pages**: overview, blocks, sources

### `finance`

Rich local finance workspace for self and parent-managed accounts.

- **reads**: tables: finance_accounts, finance_transactions, finance_holdings, finance_daily_cashflow, finance_daily_net_worth, finance_parent_draws, finance_categories, finance_transaction_user_categories, finance_categorized_transactions, enrichment_latest, enrichment_runs, enrichment_evidence, enrichment_jobs; models: burn_rate, categorize
- **writes** (via `/api/v1/apps/finance/actions/<action>`): tables: app_finance_reviews, app_finance_burn_rules, app_finance_burn_overrides, app_finance_burn_buckets, finance_categories, finance_transaction_user_categories, enrichment_jobs; actions: mark_reviewed, clear_review, set_transaction_category, clear_transaction_category, set_burn_classification, clear_burn_override, create_burn_bucket, set_burn_bucket_color, rerun_receipt_enrichment
- **pages**: overview, self, parents, review, rules, receipts, settings

### `places`

Local-first location timeline, OpenStreetMap views, and life rhythm analysis from mobile exports.

- **reads**: tables: location_points, raw_locations, geocoded_locations, daily_locations
- **writes** (via `/api/v1/apps/places/actions/<action>`): tables: app_places_settings, app_places_aliases; actions: set_privacy, set_place_alias, clear_place_alias
- **pages**: overview, map, timeline, rhythm, privacy

### `subscriptions`

Subscription spend, billing periods, and observed app/browser utilization.

- **reads**: tables: subscription_entities, subscription_charges, subscription_usage_evidence, subscription_utilization_periods, subscription_match_rules, finance_categorized_transactions
- **writes** (via `/api/v1/apps/subscriptions/actions/<action>`): tables: subscription_entities, subscription_charges, subscription_usage_evidence, subscription_utilization_periods, finance_categories, finance_transaction_user_categories, app_finance_burn_rules, app_finance_burn_buckets, ui_notes, ui_note_refs; actions: mark_not_subscription, create_note
- **pages**: overview, subscriptions, evidence, rules

## Sources (1)

### `spark_email`

Live email, calendar, contacts, and meeting access through Spark Desktop CLI

- **provider**: `spark`
- **platform: macOS only**
- **capabilities**: accounts, folders, emails, search, thread, contacts, calendar, meetings
